content:
IVX HOLDINGS — FINAL WATCHDOG TIMEOUT ROOT-CAUSE ELIMINATION

OBJECTIVE

Fix the IVX Owner AI timeout permanently across every message-send path.

Current real-device failure:

IVX AI BLOCKED — TIMEOUT
AI_TRIGGER_DECISION passed
AI_MUTATION_STARTED failed
Timed out after 180000 ms
File: expo/app/ivx/chat.tsx
Function: assistantReplyMutation.mutationFn
Reported condition:
assistantReplyMutation.mutate() was not invoked or progress was not recorded.

Do not provide another narrative report.

Inspect, fix, rebuild, deploy and provide a directly downloadable APK.

==================================================
PHASE 1 — VERIFY THE DEVICE BUILD
==================================================

Determine whether the screenshot came from:

- Build 8
- Build 10
- Another cached or older build

Add a permanently visible diagnostic in the Owner AI screen showing:

- App version
- versionCode
- Git commit SHA
- Build timestamp
- API environment
- Bundle identifier

Return the exact build running on the device.

Do not assume Build 10 is installed.

==================================================
PHASE 2 — AUDIT EVERY SEND ROOT
==================================================

Trace every possible route that can send a user message or trigger AI.

Audit all of these:

1. Send button
2. Keyboard submit
3. Voice submit
4. Attachment submit
5. Quick action button
6. Deal Review
7. Investor Reply
8. Doc Summary
9. Owner command
10. Retry button
11. Offline queued message
12. Reconnected queued message
13. Local-first chat mode
14. AI-only mode
15. Non-AI command branch
16. Fire-and-forget path
17. Mutation success callback
18. Mutation error callback
19. Mutation settled callback
20. Component remount/restart path

Every route must call one shared orchestrator.

Create one function such as:

executeOwnerAiRequest()

No individual route may directly manage watchdog state independently.

==================================================
PHASE 3 — SINGLE EXECUTION STATE MACHINE
==================================================

Implement one explicit state machine:

IDLE
→ USER_MESSAGE_ACCEPTED
→ AI_TRIGGER_DECISION
→ AI_MUTATION_STARTED
→ HTTP_REQUEST_STARTED
→ HTTP_RESPONSE_RECEIVED
→ RESPONSE_PERSISTED
→ UI_RENDERED
→ SUCCESS

Failure states:

VALIDATION_FAILED
AUTH_FAILED
NETWORK_FAILED
PROVIDER_FAILED
TIMEOUT
CANCELLED

Requirements:

- Every state transition must be recorded synchronously.
- Every transition must include traceId.
- No transition may depend only on an async callback.
- The watchdog must start only after AI_MUTATION_STARTED.
- The watchdog must stop on success, error, cancellation or component unmount.
- A request may have only one active watchdog.
- Duplicate send attempts must be idempotent.

==================================================
PHASE 4 — REMOVE FIRE-AND-FORGET FAILURE
==================================================

Search for and eliminate:

void triggerAIWithRetry(...)
triggerAIWithRetry(...).then(...)
unawaited promises
empty catch blocks
catch(() => {})
Promise calls without await
mutation calls hidden inside state callbacks
setTimeout-based mutation triggering
conditional paths that skip mutation
returns before mutation starts

All AI execution paths must be awaited and wrapped with:

try
catch
finally

The finally block must always stop or update the watchdog.

==================================================
PHASE 5 — MUTATION INVOCATION GUARANTEE
==================================================

Before invoking the mutation, synchronously record:

AI_MUTATION_STARTED

Then invoke exactly one of:

await assistantReplyMutation.mutateAsync(payload)

or an equivalent awaited request function.

Do not use mutate() for this critical execution path unless completion is wrapped in a reliable Promise.

Verify:

- Mutation function is defined
- Component is mounted
- Required conversation ID exists
- Message ID exists
- Owner session exists
- Request payload is valid
- No early return occurs
- No disabled flag prevents invocation
- No stale closure references old mutation state

==================================================
PHASE 6 — TIMEOUT DESIGN
==================================================

Replace the single 180-second silent timeout with staged timeout handling:

Stage 1:
15 seconds — show “Still working”

Stage 2:
45 seconds — retry safe network request once if no HTTP request began

Stage 3:
90 seconds — request status from backend by traceId

Stage 4:
180 seconds — fail gracefully with exact root cause

The app must never remain on an infinite spinner.

On timeout show:

- Trace ID
- Last successful checkpoint
- Exact failed checkpoint
- Network state
- HTTP request started: YES/NO
- HTTP status if available
- Retry count
- Build/version information
- A Retry button
- A Cancel button

==================================================
PHASE 7 — BACKEND IDEMPOTENCY AND STATUS
==================================================

Add or verify:

POST /api/ivx/owner-ai/request
GET /api/ivx/owner-ai/request/:traceId/status
POST /api/ivx/owner-ai/request/:traceId/retry
POST /api/ivx/owner-ai/request/:traceId/cancel

Each request must have:

- traceId
- messageId
- conversationId
- idempotency key
- createdAt
- status
- provider request ID
- response status
- error code

Repeated sends with the same idempotency key must not create duplicate AI responses.

==================================================
PHASE 8 — NETWORK AND LIFECYCLE ROOTS
==================================================

Test all of these conditions:

1. Wi-Fi
2. 5G
3. Wi-Fi to 5G switch
4. 5G to Wi-Fi switch
5. Airplane mode interruption
6. App backgrounded during request
7. App resumed during request
8. Screen rotation
9. Component remount
10. App restart
11. Backend restart
12. AI provider timeout
13. Supabase delay
14. Slow network
15. Duplicate tap
16. Conversation missing
17. Expired session
18. Owner authorization failure
19. Attachment upload still pending
20. Voice transcription still pending

No condition may produce a false “AI blocked” banner without the exact root cause.

==================================================
PHASE 9 — WATCHDOG CORRECTNESS
==================================================

Audit the watchdog implementation itself.

Verify:

- Timer is created once per request
- Timer is cleared on every terminal state
- Timer does not survive component unmount
- Timer does not survive successful completion
- Old trace IDs cannot update new requests
- A previous request cannot trigger a banner in the current request
- Persisted watchdog state expires safely
- App restart does not restore an obsolete timeout
- Clock/time calculations use monotonic elapsed time
- UI reads the active request only

Add automated tests for stale watchdog state and orphaned timers.

==================================================
PHASE 10 — TEST MATRIX
==================================================

Create automated tests for all send roots.

Minimum tests:

- 20 send-path unit tests
- 10 mutation lifecycle tests
- 10 watchdog tests
- 10 network recovery tests
- 5 component remount tests
- 5 duplicate-send tests
- 5 backend idempotency tests
- 5 timeout/retry tests

Run:

- TypeScript check
- Lint
- Unit tests
- Integration tests
- Android release build
- Bundle inspection

No skipped tests.

==================================================
PHASE 11 — SHIPPED APK VERIFICATION
==================================================

Generate a new release:

Version: 1.4.3
versionCode: 11 or higher

Do not reuse Build 10.

Extract the actual Android bundle from the final APK and verify that it contains:

- Shared executeOwnerAiRequest orchestrator
- AI_MUTATION_STARTED checkpoint
- Awaited mutation invocation
- Watchdog cleanup
- Retry logic
- Build/version diagnostic
- No obsolete timeout branch
- No fire-and-forget AI call

Also verify the old defective strings and branches are absent where they should be removed.

==================================================
PHASE 12 — PRODUCTION SYNCHRONIZATION
==================================================

Verify:

GitHub HEAD SHA
=
Render deployed SHA
=
/version SHA
=
APK embedded SHA

If any value differs, report:

RELEASE MISMATCH

and correct it before delivery.

==================================================
PHASE 13 — REQUIRED REAL APK DELIVERY
==================================================

Return the actual APK as a downloadable attachment or public HTTPS URL.

Required filename:

ivx-holdings-v1.4.3-build11.apk

Do not return:

- /home/user/...
- /tmp/...
- sandbox-only paths
- screenshots
- reports without the APK

Return:

- APK attachment or direct HTTPS link
- SHA-256
- versionCode
- Git SHA
- Build timestamp
- File size
- Signing verification

==================================================
PHASE 14 — FINAL EVIDENCE
==================================================

Return only:

1. Root causes found
2. Every send path audited
3. Files changed
4. Tests executed
5. Test pass/fail totals
6. Commit SHA
7. Deployment ID
8. Production /health
9. Production /version
10. APK embedded SHA
11. APK filename
12. Direct APK download URL or attachment
13. Remaining issues

FINAL ACCEPTANCE

This task is complete only when:

- Every send root uses one orchestrator
- assistantReplyMutation is always invoked or an exact failure is returned
- No fire-and-forget AI execution remains
- No stale watchdog timers remain
- Retry and cancellation work
- Network switching works
- App background/resume works
- Duplicate taps do not duplicate responses
- Production and APK commits match
- Build 11 or higher is generated
- The APK is directly downloadable
- Real-device testing no longer shows the timeout banner

Do not claim the issue is fixed only because checkpoint strings exist in the bundle.

The real acceptance test is successful execution on the installed APK.
